title: Sour Chewy Turkish Duck Legs Casserole
description: A hearty, nostalgic Turkish dish featuring crispy-skinned duck legs in a tangy, chewy sauce. This recipe is easily adapted to be anti-inflammatory-friendly by omitting ingredients high in inflammatory compounds.
instructions: Rinse the duck legs and pat them dry with paper towels.
Season the duck legs with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the duck legs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the duck legs from the pot and set them aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the chopped onion to the pot. Cook until the onion is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the pomegranate juice, water (if using), apple cider vinegar, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Return the duck legs to the pot and make sure they are mostly submerged in the sauce. If necessary, add a little more water to cover the duck legs.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully remove the duck legs from the sauce. Place the duck legs on a plate and cover them with foil to keep them warm.
Reduce the heat to 300°F (150°C) and continue to simmer the sauce on the stovetop or in the oven for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
To serve, place a duck leg on each plate and spoon some of the sauce over the top. Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Let the casserole rest for 5-10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
